questions.q: Rajaraja I belonged to which South Indian dynasty?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I was one of the greatest emperors of the Chola dynasty.

questions.q: Around which period did Rajaraja I reign?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I ruled roughly from 985 CE to 1014 CE, spanning the turn of the 11th century.

questions.q: Which monumental temple did Rajaraja I commission?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I built the great Brihadisvara (Rajarajeshwaram) temple at Thanjavur.

questions.q: The Brihadisvara temple built by Rajaraja I is dedicated to which deity?
questions.explanation: The Brihadisvara temple is a great Shiva temple at Thanjavur.

questions.q: Rajaraja I extended Chola power into which island to the south?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I conquered much of Sri Lanka, then known as Ila or Ilangai.

questions.q: What was the capital of Rajaraja I's Chola empire?
questions.explanation: Thanjavur (Tanjore) in the Kaveri delta was the Chola capital under Rajaraja I.

questions.q: Rajaraja I ruled mainly over which broad region of the subcontinent?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I built a powerful empire across southern India.

questions.q: What does the title 'Rajaraja' literally mean?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja means 'king of kings,' a title reflecting his imperial status.

questions.q: Who succeeded Rajaraja I and further expanded the Chola empire?
questions.explanation: His son Rajendra Chola I succeeded him and led the empire to its greatest extent.

questions.q: Rajaraja I is celebrated as a patron of which traditional South Indian art form preserved in temple bronzes?
questions.explanation: The Chola period under Rajaraja I is famed for its exquisite bronze sculptures.

questions.q: What was the personal name of Rajaraja I before he took his imperial titles?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I was originally named Arulmozhi Varman.

questions.q: Who was the father of Rajaraja I?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I was the son of Parantaka II, also known as Sundara Chola.

questions.q: Rajaraja I's conquest of Sri Lanka led him to establish a Chola provincial capital there. What name was given to the region?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja annexed northern Sri Lanka and named the province Mummudi Chola Mandalam.

questions.q: Which dynasty's territories, including the Vengi region, did Rajaraja I bring under Chola influence?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I intervened in the Eastern Chalukya succession and tied Vengi to the Cholas through alliance.

questions.q: Rajaraja I won a notable victory over a combined force of which two Tamil-region powers early in his reign?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja defeated the combined Chera and Pandya rulers, asserting Chola supremacy in the Tamil country.

questions.q: Rajaraja I is known for naval expeditions against which island group in the Indian Ocean?
questions.explanation: Chola inscriptions credit Rajaraja I with a naval campaign that subdued the Maldive islands.

questions.q: The Brihadisvara temple was completed in roughly which year, near the end of Rajaraja I's reign?
questions.explanation: The Brihadisvara temple was completed around 1010 CE, in the 25th year of his reign.

questions.q: Rajaraja I is credited with sponsoring the compilation of the hymns of the Nayanars, the Shaiva saints, into which collection?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I is traditionally credited with recovering and arranging the Shaiva hymns that form the Tirumurai canon.

questions.q: What administrative innovation marked Rajaraja I's reign, reorganising the empire into territorial units?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I systematised Chola administration into divisions such as mandalams and valanadus.

questions.q: Rajaraja I's son and successor was born to which queen, herself an important figure of the reign?
questions.explanation: Rajendra I was the son of Rajaraja's queen Vanavan Mahadevi.

questions.q: Rajaraja I's beloved elder sister, a noted patron of temples, was named what?
questions.explanation: Kundavai, Rajaraja's sister, was a prominent royal patron of religious and charitable works.

questions.q: On which river's fertile delta was the heartland of Rajaraja I's empire centred?
questions.explanation: The Chola heartland lay in the rich Kaveri river delta of Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: Which of Rajaraja I's titles refers to his supremacy over the three ancient Tamil crowns (Chola, Pandya and Chera)?
questions.explanation: Mummudi Chola ('the Chola of the three crowns') reflected Rajaraja I's mastery over the three classical Tamil kingdoms of the Cholas, Pandyas and Cheras.

questions.q: The detailed inscriptions on the walls of the Brihadisvara temple primarily record what?
questions.explanation: The temple walls bear extensive records of gold, land, livestock and personnel endowed to the temple.

questions.q: Rajaraja I's early Sri Lankan campaign sacked which then-capital of the island?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja's forces destroyed Anuradhapura, prompting the Sinhalese to shift their capital to Polonnaruwa.

questions.q: Rajaraja I extended patronage to a Buddhist monastery at Nagapattinam built for a king of which Southeast Asian realm?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja granted revenue to the Chudamani Vihara at Nagapattinam, built by the Srivijaya ruler.

questions.q: What is the regnal name often given to the Chola calendar era marking Rajaraja I's accession?
options: His accession is dated to about 985 CE
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I's reign is conventionally dated as beginning around 985 CE.

questions.q: The grand vimana (tower) of Rajaraja I's Thanjavur temple rises to approximately what height?
questions.explanation: The Brihadisvara vimana soars to roughly 66 metres, making it one of the tallest of its time.

questions.q: Rajaraja I appointed his son Rajendra as co-regent in roughly which year before his own death?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja I associated Rajendra with the throne as co-regent around 1012 CE, shortly before his death about 1014 CE.

questions.q: Which earlier Chola ruler is credited with reviving the dynasty's fortunes generations before Rajaraja I rose to power?
questions.explanation: Vijayalaya Chola revived the medieval Chola line in the 9th century, paving the way for Rajaraja I's empire.
